column1: Jeremy Pena leads the pack with a steady bat atop the order, heading into a hitter-friendly park and facing Michael Lorenzen—an ideal recipe for Beat The Streak players. Brice Turang isn’t far behind, bringing a reliable profile with a top-of-the-lineup role, a favorable park, and a clear platoon advantage against Sonny Gray. Joey Loperfido and Luis Arraez also offer strong hit chances thanks to their lineup spots and lefty matchups, but Pena’s combination of opportunity and environment puts him a cut above today.
